title: Rongali Bihu (Bohag Bihu)
intro: Rongali Bihu, also known as Bohag Bihu, is the most important and widely celebrated festival of Assam.
description:
Rongali Bihu, also known as Bohag Bihu, is the most important and widely celebrated festival of Assam. It marks the beginning of the Assamese New Year and celebrates the arrival of the spring season and the new agricultural cycle. Rongali Bihu is usually celebrated around 14 April and is associated with the harvesting season, nature, and the hopes of prosperity.

This festival is deeply connected with the agricultural traditions of Assam. Farmers express gratitude towards nature, animals, and the divine for a good harvest and a prosperous year ahead. Rongali Bihu is celebrated for several days, with Goru Bihu (the day dedicated to cattle) and Manuh Bihu (the day dedicated to people) being important parts of the celebration.

During Rongali Bihu, people wear traditional Assamese clothes, sing Bihu songs, perform the famous Bihu dance, prepare traditional food, and celebrate with family and communities. The festival reflects the rich culture, traditions, farming lifestyle, and love for nature of Assam.

Festival Month

Bohag (April)

Significance / Importance

Rongali Bihu holds great cultural, agricultural, and social importance in Assam. It celebrates the Assamese New Year, the arrival of spring, and the beginning of a new farming season.

The festival honors the hard work of farmers and expresses gratitude towards nature for providing food and prosperity. Rongali Bihu promotes happiness, unity, cultural pride, respect for nature, and new beginnings.

Celebration / Rituals

1. Clean and decorate homes before the festival.
2. Take care of cattle and worship them on Goru Bihu.
3. Express gratitude towards nature and the divine.
4. Prepare traditional Assamese dishes.
5. Participate in Bihu songs and dance celebrations.
6. Meet family members and exchange greetings.
7. Pray for happiness, prosperity, and a successful year ahead.

Related Katha / Mantra / Aarti / Chalisa

* Bihu Folk Stories
* Assamese New Year Traditions
* Surya Mantra
* Gayatri Mantra
* Nature Worship Traditions

faq.a1: Rongali Bihu is usually celebrated around 14 April every year.
faq.a2: Rongali Bihu is celebrated to welcome the Assamese New Year, spring season, and the new agricultural cycle.
faq.q3: Where is Rongali Bihu mainly celebrated?
faq.a3: Rongali Bihu is mainly celebrated in Assam.
faq.a4: Bohag Bihu is another name for Rongali Bihu and marks the beginning of the Assamese New Year.
faq.a5: Bihu dance is performed to express happiness, celebrate culture, and welcome the new harvest season.
faq.a6: Goru Bihu is the day when cattle are bathed, cared for, and honored because they play an important role in agricultural life.
faq.q7: In which month is Rongali Bihu celebrated?
faq.a7: Rongali Bihu is celebrated in the month of April.
faq.q8: What is the spiritual significance of Rongali Bihu?
faq.a8: Rongali Bihu symbolizes gratitude towards nature, unity, prosperity, cultural heritage, and the celebration of new beginnings.
